# UC-22.59.16 · IACS UR E27 OT-equipment cyber-resilience attestation register — equipment-level evidence chain

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Each critical piece of equipment on a ship — its engines, navigation, cargo controls — needs a cyber-safety certificate from the maker. We keep a list of every piece and its certificate, and we shout if any equipment is missing one or running software that doesn't match what was approved.*

---

## Description

Tracks every E27-in-scope OT component against the vendor / class-society attestation register; raises an alert when no attestation exists, the installed firmware drifts from the attested version, or the attestation revision is outdated.

## Value

E27 is the equipment-level counterpart of E26. Firmware drift between the attested baseline and the installed equipment is the single most common audit finding; this UC closes the gap.

## Implementation

(1) Build the E27 attestation KV Store. (2) Wire the supply-chain / engineering department to register every OT component delivery against UR E27 scope. (3) Compare the OT-discovery feed against the attested firmware. (4) Schedule UC-22.59.16 daily; route P3 for drift findings.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.16` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `iacs_clause="IACS-UR-E27-r3"`. Every row pins (imo_number, equipment_id, equipment_type, vendor, model, e27_revision, attestation_id, attestation_at, status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A propulsion-control unit installed under UR E26 scope shows no E27 attestation 14 days after commissioning. UC-22.59.16 MUST flag status=breach-no-attestation.

### Negative scenario

An OEM-provided E27 attestation is current and the installed firmware matches the attested revision. UC-22.59.16 MUST record status=compliant.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.3, UC-22.59.7, and UC-22.59.15 in production.
- OT-discovery sensor coverage on every vessel.

## Step 1 — E27 attestation KV Store

```ini
[iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.imo_number = string
field.equipment_id = string
field.attestation_id = string
field.e27_revision = string
field.attested_firmware = string
field.attestation_at = time
field.surveyor = string
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.16: IACS UR E27 Equipment Attestation Register]
cron_schedule = 0 5 * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.16,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IACS-UR-E27-r3
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Maritime-specific notes

- **Type approval vs ship-specific attestation**: some vendors hold a class-society type approval that covers a model family; the register must map type-approval → equipment instance.
- **End-of-life firmware**: when a vendor declares EOL on an attested firmware, the company must plan an upgrade campaign; this UC flags the trigger event.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup imo_crar_components_lookup
| where e27_in_scope = "true"
| lookup iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup imo_number equipment_id OUTPUT attestation_id e27_revision attestation_at attested_firmware
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval status = case(
    isnull(attestation_id), "breach-no-attestation",
    isnotnull(attested_firmware) AND installed_firmware != attested_firmware, "breach-firmware-mismatch",
    e27_revision != "Rev.3", "breach-revision-mismatch",
    1=1, "compliant")
| where status NOT IN ("compliant")
| sort 0 imo_number equipment_id
| table imo_number equipment_id equipment_type vendor model installed_firmware attested_firmware attestation_id e27_revision status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Fleet E27 equipment attestation table. (Panel 2) Firmware-drift count per vessel. (Panel 3) Top vendors by attestation gaps. (Panel 4) Attestation-revision mix.

## Known False Positives

1. **Firmware-version aliasing**: the OEM ships v3.4.1 but the OT-discovery sensor reports v3.4.1-build42; align the comparison logic to ignore build-suffix differences. 2. **Replacement spare parts**: an emergency engine-control card swap may briefly create an attestation gap; honour a 14-day grace window before raising P3.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_16_exceptions` with the Company Security Officer documented approval.

## References

- [IACS UR E27 — Cyber Resilience of Systems and Equipment, Rev.3 (2024)](https://iacs.org.uk/publications/unified-requirements/)
- [IEC 61162-460 — Maritime navigation and radiocommunication equipment and systems — Digital interfaces](https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/61292)
